## Legislative Update for Week Ending June 2, 2023
---
| The 2023 Unicameral Session has come to an end.

In a future newsletter we will present an overarching recap of the entire session. Our Legislative Plan committees have accomplished unprecedented research and implementation of the SCC vision. We appreciate their dedication, insight, and desire to build a network across Nebraska that seeks to improve the lives of the Nebraska people through better involvement and legislation. The 2nd House, the engaged citizens of the Republican party, established a historic level of action through testifying in person, via phone or email. Bravo and sincerely, thank you to the many 100’s of Republicans who made the trip to Lincoln or participated in the dozens of zoom meetings. It is because of ALL Republicans that this session had generational WINS and long lasting legislation to protect Nebraska.

Today, we are celebrating a change of the environment, a new direction in the entirety of a Unicameral Session. Having a vision, a Legislative Plan, greatly increased the ability for Republican voices to be a part of the process; as we should be. Our NEGOP Constitution states the objective of the party is “the maintenance of the government of, by, and for the people...and the furtherance of such principles as may from time to time be adopted”. Nowhere in the NEGOP constitution does it limit the influence of the membership (YOU) or the party to solely electing Republicans. You, the 2nd House, have a voice beyond elections and campaigns. Your voice influenced legislation. Lobbyists and consultants have for too long controlled the path and narrative of a Unicameral Session. Even staff at the Unicameral can have a greater impact on a senator’s vote than the very constituents that helped campaign for a senator. Some lobbyists, consultants, and Unicameral staff do NOT share Republican values or care only for the furtherance of money and power. To that thought...this week was not the best of weeks at the Unicameral. Bad legislation was passed. Unity faded. Tensions ran high and tempers flared between senators. Truly, prayers for grace are needed. Prayers and time to decompress from the most impactful, yet heightened session ever are needed. Many at the Unicameral were simply tired from a highly emotionally charged session and some of this spilled out over the last two weeks. As we stated in last week’s newsletter, we were concerned with some of the issues that could get through due to the fatigue that was setting in. That took place this week.

So for now, we will summarize the activity of the week in “Traditional” fashion to keep you up to date and again, will present a full session recap down the road.

Let’s dig in....

---
|
## LB50 (Public Safety)
---
| As discussed in last week’s newsletter, we were concerned with the direction of this bill since the influence of this bill migrated away from resigned Senator Suzanne Geist (Republican) to Senator Justin Wayne (Democrat). Law enforcement entities were supporting the bill because of certain amendments. We recognize these amendments WOULD give resources to police. We would be in full support of these items IF they were in a stand alone bill. However, parole board members, county attorneys, and prosecutors had very serious concerns on the main component of LB50, a reduction to our overcrowded prison system through a new early parole process. Our [Party Platform](https://mcshanellc.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=1d82dcd2e67c4075340c26b37&id=c0611c29aa&e=25844e23a8) supports the work of law enforcement across the state. Alternative punishment and rehabilitation for first-time, nonviolent offenders should be supported. However, community safety MUST be a primary focus of legislation when dealing with our judicial system or prison overcrowding, and thus providing a new parole process to inmates who are dangerous to public safety is NOT a position we can support. Specifically for Republican Senators, they were split on the issue - 15 Republicans voted against and 17 Republicans voted for the bill.

The concern is there will be some violent incidents in the future from offenders who will be released early. Recent Nebraska history has shown this to be reality. Past victims' safety and/or sanity is another concern that only time will reveal outcomes. Unfortunately, the good in this bill (which there is some) could have been adopted on a standalone basis with our full support. Proponents believe the resources for law enforcement and the other provisions in the bill outweighed the risk of lowering the sentence time. Discussion needs to occur and be applied to next year’s session on bill combinations.
---
|
---
|
## Voter ID
---
| Senator Julie Slama prepared filibusters on a Voter ID bill that was drafted out of committee, not an originally submitted bill from either her or Senator Steve Erdman. The committee didn’t even bother to have a committee hearing on LB230, yet we are being asked to respect the committee process. Normally, we do respect the process, but when a bill with endless hours invested into its creation is never discussed on the issue, we cannot support that process. LB230 needed more attention from the committee. The Senators gave ZERO time during the testifying process on this bill. This is one reason a poor bill was achieved.

Senator Slama’s primary challenges on her bill that was ultimately taken over by the committee and Secretary of State Bob Evnen, were centered around unconstitutional provisions with the US Constitution and the “will” of the Nebraska voters not being followed. The NEGOP subcommittee on Election Accountability and Voter Verification made up of SCC members of the party did not support this final bill as written. The subcommittee agrees this bill did not follow the Nebraska Constitution as written. The main argument being...the photo identification shall be presented by the qualified voter before the ballot is deposited (cast) in the box...was not being observed.

Despite these Constitutional objections, the bill was overwhelmingly approved by the senators. From our perspective, the body on the whole didn’t want a special session. In fairness, several senators mentioned on the mic they were more than willing to come back and address the language. The delay in dealing with this issue earlier in the session really hurt the cause. The dismissal of discussing the stronger alternative legislation is very telling of where the power players are on this issue.

As we stated when we were elected, we will uphold the Constitution (both Federal and State). This bill does not meet the standards of the 65.45% of voters who approved the Constitutional amendment language as written.

We have been guided by Speaker Arch that this bill can be cleaned up in future years. We hope he is correct. Speaker Arch has kept his word with us the whole session (even if we did disagree on a few issues), so I’m more than willing to give him the benefit of the doubt. Fixing problems in later sessions is often promised...but never happens. We believe the best way to correct a bad bill is to not pass it to begin with, and we are saddened this philosophy is not held by our senators. Encourage your senator to become better educated on the issue in 2023, study the Lincoln primary elections, and commit to a change of legislation in 2024. Constitutional America is in jeopardy due to insecure elections.

On a final note, as I mentioned in the newsletter two editions ago I met with Secretary of State Evnen on the concerns of early voting. As your political director, I was HIGHLY concerned with what I saw in Lincoln on “Early Voting”. For full disclosure, I do not trust the results of the early vote in Lincoln. The statistical probability of those correlated results are suspiciously similar to 4 years ago and the normal correlations between early voting and game day voting did not occur. In fairness, I gave Secretary of State Evnen a chance to disclose how he feels on early voting two weeks ago. This is what I received from Secretary Evnen on Saturday morning, unedited, to help the reader understand where he is coming from on the issue.

>From Secretary of State Bob Evnen...

_“I am no fan of no-excuse early voting by mail and drop box and I have consistently said so. When I testified before the Senate Rules Committee in March, I said that these methods of early voting pose the greatest challenge to maintaining election security. That’s because early voters by mail and drop box are much more vulnerable to undue influence and manipulation, and because the ballots themselves are more vulnerable to wrongful handling by third parties.

The problem is that no-excuse early voting has been going on in our state for 18 years, and something on the order of 250,000 Nebraskans like to vote early using these methods. Ending this method of voting is likely to be poorly received by a lot of Nebraskans.

I have requested legislation over the past few years to narrow and better secure early ballot methods. Pending before the legislature this year is LB 390, sponsored by Senator Clements at my request, which is a measure to further limit early voting abuses. It has ten cosponsors, including Senator Slama. It is pending on General File.

Using voter ID to make early voting more difficult is likely to suppress voting in the Third District and the rural First District; why would we ever want to do that?

If you want to get rid of early voting, bring a bill to do it. Disguising this objective in other legislation has secondary effects that we don’t want.”_
---
|
---
|
## Veto Overrides
---
| State Auditor Foley found his request for more funds to retain his staff restored by the legislature. The Governor had vetoed his request for funds to shore up a staffing problem. In 2023, the State Auditor has cited many concerning reports happening with state agencies and has been effective in highlighting noncompliance in many areas. We have found the work of his office valuable to the taxpayer and in need of support.

The Governor vetoes that were upheld were on housing and provider reimbursement rates. Vigorous debate was had on these issues. However, neither issue had the votes to overcome the veto.
---
|
---
|
## Governor Pillen Stands Up To China
---
| The NEGOP appreciates Governor Pillen for a significant change of tone on China. With President Biden doing little to protect us (how about those spy balloons given free range across the continent?), Governor Pillen highlighted in his closing speech that Huawei (Chinese based) hardware will be banned in Nebraska cell towers.

An enumerated role the US Constitution DOES call the federal government to execute is - to provide for the common defense. Unfortunately, this isn’t happening to the level it should be based on a lack of directives from the White House and Congress. This inaction is creating gaps in our national defense. We are grateful the state is stepping up to fill the void where they can to protect Nebraskans. The more the state can do to protect us from the influences of the Chinese Communist Party or any other outside influence, the better. Look for more legislation in the coming years to further protect the good life from the influence of the CCP.
---
|
---
|
## E-15 requirement approved for retailers
---
| Beginning in 2024, new retailers of fuel (or those who replace 80% of their pumps), will be required to have half of their pumps dedicated to selling ethanol based fuels. Retailers will receive eight cents a gallon in state tax credits (up to $5 million) for their sale of these fuels.
---
|
---
|
## ACLU and Planned Parenthood files lawsuit against #LB574 (abortion and gender mutilation bill)
---
| The ACLU filed a lawsuit to strike down LB574. The left continues to fight against this legislation at all costs. The Attorney General asked the court to reject the injunction. Stay tuned as this suit progresses in court.
---
|
---
|
## The Education Lobby files a formal petition to stop Opportunity Scholarships
---
| The NSEA filed a petition to overturn the Opportunity Scholarships Bill. The education lobbies who received a $1 billion dollar education future fund and over $300 million in additional funding fired missiles against the funds allocated to opportunity scholarships. As we have mentioned in the prior letters, the education lobbies are used to getting their way all day, everyday, and do not compromise to help other parents struggling with their child to find the right education they need. This year was a shock to their system as they finally lost on an issue and felt some resistance. Can you imagine filing a suit against the wishes of those who just gave you a billion dollars out of a limited state budget? We can’t. They can. We will continue to work for all students in Nebraska who need choices to improve their lives, enhance opportunities, and empower themselves for a better tomorrow.
---
|
---
|
## DC Debt Limits and the Subject of Default
---
| The US House of Representatives reached a deal to raise the debt ceiling. The House Freedom Caucus led an effort to defeat the compromised deal. Democrats had their own contingent that were unhappy with the deal because it did not go far enough to the left in “preserving” spending and they voted against this bill as well. In the end, 71 of the 222 Republicans voted against the deal in the House; the Nebraska House delegation voted in entirety for the compromise.

Once the deal reached the Senate, the Nebraska Senators joined 34 other Republicans and voted against the compromise. Final count, the Nebraska House delegation voted “For” and the Nebraska Senate delegation “Against” raising the debt limit. We are grateful our state is not in debt and has set the example of balancing our budget at home. DC should follow our lead. Furthermore, we are grateful for the collaboration on many issues across our party leadership across the many states.

On Friday, the Idaho Chairwoman penned the following letter on the debt deal. It is well written and we believe match the sentiments of many across Nebraska. We are providing her comments unedited.

Thank you Idaho....
---
|
## There is no victory in debt ceiling deal
---
| **By Dorothy Moon, IDGOP Chairwoman**

_“The United States is grappling with a severe spending addiction that shows no signs of abating. Year after year, our government fails to implement responsible budgetary measures, instead opting to borrow more money to fund its excessive expenditures. The recent decision to raise the debt ceiling, while it may help us avoid immediate disaster, does little to change the trajectory of government growth. While some felt this was necessary under the circumstances, it is crucial to acknowledge that this action only perpetuates the continued expansion of government and once again exposes the reckless spending habits of our federal government, enabling this addiction to persist and burdening future generations with insurmountable debt.

As we move past this recent debt ceiling issue, it is essential to focus on the actual problem at hand: our government's routine and significant overspending. The fundamental issue lies in the fact that our country consistently spends far more than it takes in. This is simply unsustainable and threatens the long-term economic stability and prosperity of our nation.

The State of Idaho, like many others, has been accepting funds from the empty pocket of the federal government for years. It is crucial to recognize that the government, just like any individual or household, cannot continue to borrow indefinitely without dire consequences. It is the equivalent to using a credit card with no spending limit—it may temporarily create a false sense of security, but it only postpones the inevitable. We must acknowledge that spending beyond our means is not a sustainable solution.

The pattern of kicking the can down the road, masquerading it as a major victory, is nothing more than an illusion. By deferring the debt crisis to the future, we are prolonging the inevitable and potentially worsening the consequences. Instead of addressing our financial challenges head-on, our leaders are burdening future generations with the responsibility of rectifying our fiscal irresponsibility. It is disheartening to witness such claims of success while avoiding the fundamental issue at hand.

Conservative Republicans fully understand the significance of fiscal responsibility and find it unacceptable to continuously spend money we do not have. While the recent debt ceiling increase may have been the best course of action in the immediate term, it does little to address the underlying problem. As Republicans, we advocate for a more conservative approach to our nation's finances. We recognize that reducing spending is a challenging but necessary task. It is imperative that we focus on minimizing government waste, promoting economic growth, and implementing prudent budgeting practices.

Now more than ever, it is crucial to petition our leaders to implement responsible fiscal policies that prioritize the long-term well-being of our nation. We must remain aware of the dangers of spending beyond our means and the severe consequences it imposes on our children and grandchildren. By confronting our financial challenges head-on, we can safeguard the prosperity of our nation and secure a better future for all Americans.”_

Thank you Chairwoman Moon....
---
|
## Regular Business Passes
---
| This was an unprecedented year. Combining numerous bills into one piece of legislation due to the filibuster nightmare defines 2023 diplomacy. This week saw many of those large bills around a single topic passed.

Economic development, banking, insurance, education, healthcare, and transportation bills were all passed. We would need 100 pages of written material to cover all the subjects in these bills. We would point you to various news outlets to learn the specific provisions combined into these large omnibus bills to learn about the specific issue that concerns you the most.

The party was in support and opposition to various bills (at the beginning of introduction) that were combined into one big bill in their given subject area. Therefore we had no “For” or “Against” on many of these bills on a formal level at the end of the session. We had split opinions on various provisions across our various subcommittees made up of governing body members.

As a point of clarity, we will be pushing hard for a return to a pre-2023 legislative process in future years. We believe Senator Halloran’s, Erdman’s, and Hansen’s ideas around a bill limit (a range of 6 to 12 per Senator) has significant merit. The amount of information being asked to be consumed by all parties involved is too enormous. No senator had the time or ability to gain knowledge on many of these bills. That fact alone, should be a danger sign to citizens. It's not criticizing Senators for the lack of knowledge on all the bills as the NEGOP team struggled to keep up with all the information in 800+ bills as well. Furthermore, these “Omnibus” bills will create “Big Government” if we continue down this road. Discussion on Unicameral rule ideas will be presented to the Central Committee for formal approval before we formally advocate for these ideas in future sessions. If you read the 2022 Legislative plan you will find we were massively successful in implementing the agenda. However, if you examine it closely you will see the biggest area we failed on was rules (not without trying).

Quality over Quantity when it comes to Legislation. More single value/merit bills over horse trading consolidation bills.
---
|
## The Governor Presents His Choice to Lead the National Guard
---
| The National Guard experienced a decimation in numbers when President Biden put vaccine requirements over this force. Many members of our National Guard quit over these unconstitutional mandates on an experimental vaccine. This once abundant force has been a fraction of its former self since these actions. The NEGOP lobbied Senator Brewer’s bill to address the employment issues caused by government mandates over a NEBRASKA force.

Brigadier General Craig Strong was nominated by the Governor to be approved by the Legislature. If approved, the General will have his work cut out to rebuild our Guard to the stronger force it once was before the vaccine mandate decimation.

We believe the National Guard is critical to law and order and provides great emergency services to the state and beyond. We will support whoever is appointed in their efforts to return this body to the strength it once possessed.

---
|
## Summary
---
| Readers...be encouraged, but not satisfied! In sports terms, we had a winning season! We won some big games! Yet, we can still improve our record. Overall, the trajectory of the legislature changed in 2023. We were proud to be a part of the change. The future of our country remains at stake if we don’t fix our elections, our schools, and our backyard (an amazing start in 2023). I anticipate these areas will be of continued emphasis as we move forward with our traditional core Republican values in our platform.

As we transition back into elections...take note, the Lincoln municipality races gave a template on election problems, most likely in the voter verification process (this is why Photo Voter ID is key). Leaders in the NEGOP need to stay diligent and take a hard look before the next primary at the campaign process and procedures of our elections.

Until the next letter...Give praise, hug your family, appreciate our land, and enjoy the great state of Nebraska! I look forward to seeing many of you on my Nebraska listening tour this summer. Kearney on Monday and Lexington on Tuesday are my first stops. See some of you then!
---
| Todd Watson
Political Director
NEGOP
---

clean_body: | | | | | | | Peter, | Legislative Update for Week Ending June 2, 2023 LB50 (Public Safety) Voter ID Veto Overrides Governor Pillen Stands Up To China E-15 requirement approved for retailers ACLU and Planned Parenthood files lawsuit against #LB574 (abortion and gender mutilation bill) The Education Lobby files a formal petition to stop Opportunity Scholarships DC Debt Limits and the Subject of Default There is no victory in debt ceiling deal Regular Business Passes The Governor Presents His Choice to Lead the National Guard Summary | | Legislative Update for Week Ending June 2, 2023 | The 2023 Unicameral Session has come to an end. In a future newsletter we will present an overarching recap of the entire session. Our Legislative Plan committees have accomplished unprecedented research and implementation of the SCC vision. We appreciate their dedication, insight, and desire to build a network across Nebraska that seeks to improve the lives of the Nebraska people through better involvement and legislation. The 2nd House, the engaged citizens of the Republican party, established a historic level of action through testifying in person, via phone or email. Bravo and sincerely, thank you to the many 100’s of Republicans who made the trip to Lincoln or participated in the dozens of zoom meetings. It is because of ALL Republicans that this session had generational WINS and long lasting legislation to protect Nebraska. Today, we are celebrating a change of the environment, a new direction in the entirety of a Unicameral Session. Having a vision, a Legislative Plan, greatly increased the ability for Republican voices to be a part of the process; as we should be. Our NEGOP Constitution states the objective of the party is “the maintenance of the government of, by, and for the people...and the furtherance of such principles as may from time to time be adopted”. Nowhere in the NEGOP constitution does it limit the influence of the membership (YOU) or the party to solely electing Republicans. You, the 2nd House, have a voice beyond elections and campaigns. Your voice influenced legislation. Lobbyists and consultants have for too long controlled the path and narrative of a Unicameral Session. Even staff at the Unicameral can have a greater impact on a senator’s vote than the very constituents that helped campaign for a senator. Some lobbyists, consultants, and Unicameral staff do NOT share Republican values or care only for the furtherance of money and power. To that thought...this week was not the best of weeks at the Unicameral. Bad legislation was passed. Unity faded. Tensions ran high and tempers flared between senators. Truly, prayers for grace are needed. Prayers and time to decompress from the most impactful, yet heightened session ever are needed. Many at the Unicameral were simply tired from a highly emotionally charged session and some of this spilled out over the last two weeks. As we stated in last week’s newsletter, we were concerned with some of the issues that could get through due to the fatigue that was setting in. That took place this week. So for now, we will summarize the activity of the week in “Traditional” fashion to keep you up to date and again, will present a full session recap down the road. Let’s dig in.... | LB50 (Public Safety) | As discussed in last week’s newsletter, we were concerned with the direction of this bill since the influence of this bill migrated away from resigned Senator Suzanne Geist (Republican) to Senator Justin Wayne (Democrat). Law enforcement entities were supporting the bill because of certain amendments. We recognize these amendments WOULD give resources to police. We would be in full support of these items IF they were in a stand alone bill. However, parole board members, county attorneys, and prosecutors had very serious concerns on the main component of LB50, a reduction to our overcrowded prison system through a new early parole process. Our supports the work of law enforcement across the state. Alternative punishment and rehabilitation for first-time, nonviolent offenders should be supported. However, community safety MUST be a primary focus of legislation when dealing with our judicial system or prison overcrowding, and thus providing a new parole process to inmates who are dangerous to public safety is NOT a position we can support. Specifically for Republican Senators, they were split on the issue - 15 Republicans voted against and 17 Republicans voted for the bill. The concern is there will be some violent incidents in the future from offenders who will be released early. Recent Nebraska history has shown this to be reality. Past victims' safety and/or sanity is another concern that only time will reveal outcomes. Unfortunately, the good in this bill (which there is some) could have been adopted on a standalone basis with our full support. Proponents believe the resources for law enforcement and the other provisions in the bill outweighed the risk of lowering the sentence time. Discussion needs to occur and be applied to next year’s session on bill combinations. | | Voter ID | Senator Julie Slama prepared filibusters on a Voter ID bill that was drafted out of committee, not an originally submitted bill from either her or Senator Steve Erdman. The committee didn’t even bother to have a committee hearing on LB230, yet we are being asked to respect the committee process. Normally, we do respect the process, but when a bill with endless hours invested into its creation is never discussed on the issue, we cannot support that process. LB230 needed more attention from the committee. The Senators gave ZERO time during the testifying process on this bill. This is one reason a poor bill was achieved. Senator Slama’s primary challenges on her bill that was ultimately taken over by the committee and Secretary of State Bob Evnen, were centered around unconstitutional provisions with the US Constitution and the “will” of the Nebraska voters not being followed. The NEGOP subcommittee on Election Accountability and Voter Verification made up of SCC members of the party did not support this final bill as written. The subcommittee agrees this bill did not follow the Nebraska Constitution as written. The main argument being...the photo identification shall be presented by the qualified voter before the ballot is deposited (cast) in the box...was not being observed. Despite these Constitutional objections, the bill was overwhelmingly approved by the senators. From our perspective, the body on the whole didn’t want a special session. In fairness, several senators mentioned on the mic they were more than willing to come back and address the language. The delay in dealing with this issue earlier in the session really hurt the cause. The dismissal of discussing the stronger alternative legislation is very telling of where the power players are on this issue. As we stated when we were elected, we will uphold the Constitution (both Federal and State). This bill does not meet the standards of the 65.45% of voters who approved the Constitutional amendment language as written. We have been guided by Speaker Arch that this bill can be cleaned up in future years. We hope he is correct. Speaker Arch has kept his word with us the whole session (even if we did disagree on a few issues), so I’m more than willing to give him the benefit of the doubt. Fixing problems in later sessions is often promised...but never happens. We believe the best way to correct a bad bill is to not pass it to begin with, and we are saddened this philosophy is not held by our senators. Encourage your senator to become better educated on the issue in 2023, study the Lincoln primary elections, and commit to a change of legislation in 2024. Constitutional America is in jeopardy due to insecure elections. On a final note, as I mentioned in the newsletter two editions ago I met with Secretary of State Evnen on the concerns of early voting. As your political director, I was HIGHLY concerned with what I saw in Lincoln on “Early Voting”. For full disclosure, I do not trust the results of the early vote in Lincoln. The statistical probability of those correlated results are suspiciously similar to 4 years ago and the normal correlations between early voting and game day voting did not occur. In fairness, I gave Secretary of State Evnen a chance to disclose how he feels on early voting two weeks ago. This is what I received from Secretary Evnen on Saturday morning, unedited, to help the reader understand where he is coming from on the issue. From Secretary of State Bob Evnen... “I am no fan of no-excuse early voting by mail and drop box and I have consistently said so. When I testified before the Senate Rules Committee in March, I said that these methods of early voting pose the greatest challenge to maintaining election security. That’s because early voters by mail and drop box are much more vulnerable to undue influence and manipulation, and because the ballots themselves are more vulnerable to wrongful handling by third parties. The problem is that no-excuse early voting has been going on in our state for 18 years, and something on the order of 250,000 Nebraskans like to vote early using these methods. Ending this method of voting is likely to be poorly received by a lot of Nebraskans. I have requested legislation over the past few years to narrow and better secure early ballot methods. Pending before the legislature this year is LB 390, sponsored by Senator Clements at my request, which is a measure to further limit early voting abuses. It has ten cosponsors, including Senator Slama. It is pending on General File. Using voter ID to make early voting more difficult is likely to suppress voting in the Third District and the rural First District; why would we ever want to do that? If you want to get rid of early voting, bring a bill to do it. Disguising this objective in other legislation has secondary effects that we don’t want.” | | Veto Overrides | State Auditor Foley found his request for more funds to retain his staff restored by the legislature. The Governor had vetoed his request for funds to shore up a staffing problem. Vigorous debate was had on these issues. However, neither issue had the votes to overcome the veto. | | Governor Pillen Stands Up To China | The NEGOP appreciates Governor Pillen for a significant change of tone on China. With President Biden doing little to protect us (how about those spy balloons given free range across the continent?), Governor Pillen highlighted in his closing speech that Huawei (Chinese based) hardware will be banned in Nebraska cell towers. An enumerated role the US Constitution DOES call the federal government to execute is - to provide for the common defense. Unfortunately, this isn’t happening to the level it should be based on a lack of directives from the White House and Congress. This inaction is creating gaps in our national defense. We are grateful the state is stepping up to fill the void where they can to protect Nebraskans. The more the state can do to protect us from the influences of the Chinese Communist Party or any other outside influence, the better. Look for more legislation in the coming years to further protect the good life from the influence of the CCP. | | E-15 requirement approved for retailers | Beginning in 2024, new retailers of fuel (or those who replace 80% of their pumps), will be required to have half of their pumps dedicated to selling ethanol based fuels. Retailers will receive eight cents a gallon in state tax credits (up to $5 million) for their sale of these fuels. | | ACLU and Planned Parenthood files lawsuit against #LB574 (abortion and gender mutilation bill) | The ACLU filed a lawsuit to strike down LB574. The left continues to fight against this legislation at all costs. The Attorney General asked the court to reject the injunction. Stay tuned as this suit progresses in court. | | The Education Lobby files a formal petition to stop Opportunity Scholarships | The NSEA filed a petition to overturn the Opportunity Scholarships Bill. This year was a shock to their system as they finally lost on an issue and felt some resistance. Can you imagine filing a suit against the wishes of those who just gave you a billion dollars out of a limited state budget? We can’t. They can. We will continue to work for all students in Nebraska who need choices to improve their lives, enhance opportunities, and empower themselves for a better tomorrow. | | DC Debt Limits and the Subject of Default | The US House of Representatives reached a deal to raise the debt ceiling. Until the next letter...Give praise, hug your family, appreciate our land, and enjoy the great state of Nebraska! I look forward to seeing many of you on my Nebraska listening tour this summer. Kearney on Monday and Lexington on Tuesday are my first stops. See some of you then! | Todd Watson Political Director NEGOP | | | | | | | | | | | | "PAID FOR BY THE NEBRASKA REPUBLICAN PARTY" | | Copyright © 2023 Nebraska Republican Party, All rights reserved.

**But CBS News says this could be one of the most expensive races in the country, and with Senate control hanging in the balance, national Republicans are pouring money into Cruz's war chest. clean_body: Have you heard? | = Peter, a lot of folks told me not to run against Ted Cruz. They said a Democrat can't win a statewide election in Texas and that I should stick with my "safe" seat in the House after winning with 66% of the vote last year. But I'm not one to play it safe when our rights and values are in danger. And listen, this team raised more in the first 24 hours of our campaign than Cruz did in the entire first quarter of 2023 – and the experts are taking notice. Already, the Cook Political Report upgraded our chances of winning, and both Roll Call AND Inside Elections officially moved Texas from "Solid Republican" to "Battleground"! = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your split donation between Colin Allred and Upset the Setup will go through immediately: | = = = = = = I've never felt better about our chances. Nate Silver's FiveThirtyEight says Texas may be Democrats' best pickup opportunity next year, CNN is calling Cruz one of the most vulnerable incumbents on the ballot and, if we win in Texas, Democrats will hold the Senate Majority. But CBS News says this could be one of the most expensive races in the country, and with Senate control hanging in the balance, national Republicans are pouring money into Cruz's war chest. They've already launched their first attacks, and if we miss our fundraising goals, I won't have the resources to fight back. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your split donation between Colin Allred and Upset the Setup will go through immediately: = = = = = = Let's do this. name: Rep. Jahana Hayes
subject: I take this vote very seriously
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Jahana Hayes
body:
I want to tell you about why I voted “no” on the debt ceiling deal and why this matters so deeply to me. | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/64278873/409587276/-1295403277?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82ODk4NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwN2E2NWE3MS00NTAyLWVlMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=zA7rAzAElpDW-fdo4Xw7Ur7fWG3Fcz1_fuoRktfGtuA=&emci=8932f547-8501-ee11-907c-00224832eb73&emdi=07a65a71-4502-ee11-907c-00224832eb73&ceid=15529135) | Dear friends, As you may have heard, I voted “no” on the recent budget agreement bill, and I want to share with you why I stood up to be a "no" vote even against pressure to vote yes. The issue of food security is very important to me, and after seeing the dangerous cuts to food benefits, I could not in good conscience vote for this agreement. While I understand the potential consequences of a default, I simply cannot make this compromise on the backs of our most vulnerable citizens. Our national debt is not the fault of those on food benefits – which amount to only $6 per person per day. The national debt is a result of tax loopholes for the ultra-wealthy and large corporations who do not pay their fair share. In fact, many pay less in taxes than hardworking middle-class families. I simply cannot support a package that takes food away from our most vulnerable while failing to solve the root cause of our national debt. This agreement poses a threat to the long-term well-being of those who are already food insecure and may result in many going hungry. I will never vote for a bill that puts folks in danger. This is not a popular opinion, but I was not elected to Congress to simply follow popular opinion. I was elected to represent the people of Connecticut’s 5th district to the best of my ability and to always support what I know is right. I will continue to fight for integrity and stand up for our most vulnerable citizens in the face of powerful forces representing the elite and the wealthy. Thank you, – Jahana
---

clean_body: I want to tell you about why I voted “no” on the debt ceiling deal and why this matters so deeply to me. | | Dear friends, As you may have heard, I voted “no” on the recent budget agreement bill, and I want to share with you why I stood up to be a "no" vote even against pressure to vote yes. The issue of food security is very important to me, and after seeing the dangerous cuts to food benefits, I could not in good conscience vote for this agreement. While I understand the potential consequences of a default, I simply cannot make this compromise on the backs of our most vulnerable citizens. Our national debt is not the fault of those on food benefits – which amount to only $6 per person per day. The national debt is a result of tax loopholes for the ultra-wealthy and large corporations who do not pay their fair share. In fact, many pay less in taxes than hardworking middle-class families. I simply cannot support a package that takes food away from our most vulnerable while failing to solve the root cause of our national debt. This agreement poses a threat to the long-term well-being of those who are already food insecure and may result in many going hungry. I will never vote for a bill that puts folks in danger. This is not a popular opinion, but I was not elected to Congress to simply follow popular opinion. I was elected to represent the people of Connecticut’s 5th district to the best of my ability and to always support what I know is right. I will continue to fight for integrity and stand up for our most vulnerable citizens in the face of powerful forces representing the elite and the wealthy. Thank you, – Jahana | | About Jahana** Jahana Hayes proudly represents Connecticut's 5th Congressional district. Keep up with the latest from Jahana on and . ---|--- Email is an important way for us to keep supporters like you informed about critical issues and to build a winning grassroots campaign. Peter, this is horrifying.
Since _Roe_ __ was overturned,**24 states** have moved to ban nearly all abortion, but that's not enough for Donald Trump.
Trump and the Republican Party want patients in all 50 states to be refused potentially lifesaving abortion care – that would include here in Washington state. They want to arrest doctors across the country for providing necessary health care. And, they want the government to make personal reproductive decisions for us.
**We will not stand idly by as Trump encourages GOP politicians to take away women’s fundamental right to make their own decisions about their bodies and their futures. We hope you won't either, Peter.**

Fellow American, I intend to have a prime spot on the debate stage for the first Presidential debate in August.

I intend to elevate the debate process for the 2024 presidential primary.

I have officially qualified for the debate but we cannot afford to slow down. I also want to make sure I have a front and center spot in August. We need to continue to secure unique grassroots donations to discuss how to actually *FIX* our country.

**GET TO KNOW VIVEK:** Vivek is an American entrepreneur, political and cultural thought leader, and New York Times bestselling author of **_Woke, Inc.: Inside Corporate America's Social Justice Scam_** , along with his second book, **_Nation of Victims: Identity Politics, the Death of Merit, and the Path Back to Excellence_**.

He has been a prominent and outspoken critic against ESG, discriminatory practices under the guise of critical race theory, and of what he calls "government-tech censorship," where the government colludes with big tech to do through the backdoor what it couldn't do through the front door under the Constitution.

Vivek is a first-generation American. Growing up in the Cincinnati area, he often recounts the sage advice he received from his father that "If you're going to stand out, then you might as well be outstanding." This advice set the course for his life: an accomplished pianist, a nationally ranked tennis player, and the valedictorian of his Jesuit high school. He went on to graduate summa cum laude in Biology from Harvard in 2007 and received his J.D. degree from Yale Law School in 2013, worked at a hedge fund, then started a pharmaceutical company, Roivant Sciences, where his team developed five drugs that went on to become FDA-approved.

In 2022, he founded Strive Asset Management, an Ohio-based firm, that takes direct aim at asset managers like BlackRock, State Street, Vanguard and others for their fiduciary breach of duty to maximize the wealth of their clients for Wokeism. Strive's mission is to restore the voices of everyday citizens in the American economy by leading companies to focus on excellence over politics.

name: Freedom Nation Today
subject: The greatest armed conflict in human history
body:
Only a fraction of the 16 million Americans who fought bravely in World War II remain with us.

This sponsored message from Freedom Nation Today is on behalf of Hillsdale College [](https://links.freedomnationtoday.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=bd514ba81ab34db0b205d439d41b7be7&_e=GRKm_MKW_7dS8dKmMjQ-GfXPN591BGT7JUs5vmq0ozem2WH2tOGvInv7q16X5c7YQ0ZeH4rZkaqUyFAAabBAPJ9uJvgxNVxpHw7gPHC4yIbDZlMWN_YmUJnHu6elHD02LrXiOlTPNpJ9HFDXcAqn1gnBiZMP8cIiAYthxogDaTg4gR0MrXvIMyU0FckngzpvjSs3bnbfUFpPb3V2xnNFtl6SccOL3uqY0BFpWYra3bs3sKts1y2IVsMiW54qXzT-SCP7pyiQjGGowDYx4UCsto6YsUVONFhM-KeKRuZhZm1F3DWky6jEQzB0OskDyLi13AampM2QhLoKC0bpSuiIWmVEFCQ1ALxmVtsckZJHr3zACT98D989FGRtFi6FefTcakuQrr-4rDclDmrpJz6tNir48j11LBCaWVAk27sKb3M%3D) Dear Fellow American,
Only a fraction of the 16 million Americans who fought bravely in World War II remain with us. This fact means that we're losing more and more living memory of the greatest armed conflict in human history, which makes the study of World War II even more important.
To this end, Hillsdale College has produced an online course entitled "The Second World Wars."
Taught by historian Victor Davis Hanson, this course explores how the conflict's diverse theaters, belligerents, and ways of fighting came eventually to define a single war. And it examines the key investments, strategies, and leaders that led the Allied powers to victory over the Axis. clean_body: Only a fraction of the 16 million Americans who fought bravely in World War II remain with us. This sponsored message from Freedom Nation Today is on behalf of Hillsdale College Dear Fellow American, Only a fraction of the 16 million Americans who fought bravely in World War II remain with us. This fact means that we're losing more and more living memory of the greatest armed conflict in human history, which makes the study of World War II even more important. To this end, Hillsdale College has produced an online course entitled "The Second World Wars." Taught by historian Victor Davis Hanson, this course explores how the conflict's diverse theaters, belligerents, and ways of fighting came eventually to define a single war. And it examines the key investments, strategies, and leaders that led the Allied powers to victory over the Axis. If you'd like to secure a DVD set before they're all gone, you can get one shipped directly to your home for any gift of $100 or more to Hillsdale College. Here's a secure link to reserve your copy of "The Second World Wars " on DVD: Thank you for preserving liberty for future generations of Americans through education with Hillsdale College! Best regards, Kyle Murnen, '09 Director of Online Learning Hillsdale College Freedom Nation Today 625 First Street #183, Alexandria, VA 22314 | _

Peter,

We’re excited to have you on Team Franz! Hilary laid out why this election is so crucial to our future, and we’ll need a strong team to win.

Because you’re a new member of the team, we wanted to make sure we heard firsthand from you what’s on your mind and what priorities matter most to you.

****[So, can we count on you to take a quick 30 seconds out of your day to let Hilary know directly what your top priorities are?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/64284027/409592892/-171229370?refcode=emonboarding2_L1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS8xMDA1NzQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiODFjMzhjZWQtNDkwMi1lZTExLTkwN2MtMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=6A-ytqMnUF5M_OKBjwbwxAVQa0RMm4AnlUKA7wc4wGI=&emci=e91caff2-9ff9-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&emdi=81c38ced-4902-ee11-907c-00224832eb73&ceid=11003312)****

As commissioner of public lands, Hilary has taken bold action to address climate change. From wildfire prevention to salmon recovery, she’s drastically changed the way we preserve our state’s natural resources and protect our public lands, and her agency has created thousands of jobs along the way – showing that fighting climate change and protecting our environment can also boost our economy.

But Hilary knows one thing is clear: Our work can’t stop here. There’s still so much more to be done, and we must act together.

**As she continues to develop new strategies to tackle some of our state's biggest challenges, Hilary wants to hear directly from you, Peter. **

****[Will you take our quick, 30-second survey and let us know what issues matter most to you and your family?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/64284028/409592906/-171229370?refcode=emonboarding2_L2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS8xMDA1NzQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiODFjMzhjZWQtNDkwMi1lZTExLTkwN2MtMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=6A-ytqMnUF5M_OKBjwbwxAVQa0RMm4AnlUKA7wc4wGI=&emci=e91caff2-9ff9-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&emdi=81c38ced-4902-ee11-907c-00224832eb73&ceid=11003312)**** **[TAKE OUR SURVEY](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/64284030/409592918/-171229370?refcode=emonboarding2_B1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS8xMDA1NzQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiODFjMzhjZWQtNDkwMi1lZTExLTkwN2MtMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=6A-ytqMnUF5M_OKBjwbwxAVQa0RMm4AnlUKA7wc4wGI=&emci=e91caff2-9ff9-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&emdi=81c38ced-4902-ee11-907c-00224832eb73&ceid=11003312)**
It’ll only take a few seconds, and Hilary can’t wait to hear from you, so please, Peter, make your voice heard.

Thank you,

Team Franz

name: Jeff Merkley
subject: The color hunters wear in the woods
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jeff Merkley for Oregon
body:
| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/64284215/409593382/150430950?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDEvMS84OTMyNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmOGY2NDI5MS00ODAyLWVlMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=TCeU6CsXff93Xm-DrNGWuItI8oGYjGXYTPHO_NHa6qg=&emci=a56f40ab-4702-ee11-907c-00224832eb73&emdi=f8f64291-4802-ee11-907c-00224832eb73&ceid=7731215)
---

| | This weekend we wear orange. On January 21, 2013, Hadiya Pendleton, a high school student from Chicago, marched in President Barack Obama's second inaugural parade. A week later she was shot and killed on a playground in Chicago. After Hadiya's death, her friends commemorated her life by wearing orange. **They chose orange because it is the color hunters wear in the woods to protect themselves and others.** Wear Orange began on June 2, 2015, Hadiya's 18th birthday, and is now observed nationally on the first Friday, Saturday, and Sunday of every June. **This weekend is about solidarity, remembrance, and most importantly, _action_.** Hadiya was killed more than a decade ago and 2,000 miles from my home in Oregon, but her story feels as close as ever. Our communities live with the perpetual sorrow of gun violence. Its shadow falls on our schools, our grocery stores, and our streets. Every single day, new families receive the devastating news that a loved one has been shot and killed. **We can do more to prevent gun violence.** We can ban assault weapons and bump stocks. We can ban the production of high capacity magazines. We can close background check loopholes. And we can stand up to those who profit by encouraging bloodshed. I am committed to taking on gun violence in our country. I am committed to protecting our communities. And I am committed to working for you. **This weekend we wear orange to raise awareness of gun violence. But every day we act to try and end gun violence.** Onward! _Jeff_ | Express Donate: If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution instantly: | clean_body: | | | | This weekend we wear orange. On January 21, 2013, Hadiya Pendleton, a high school student from Chicago, marched in President Barack Obama's second inaugural parade. A week later she was shot and killed on a playground in Chicago. After Hadiya's death, her friends commemorated her life by wearing orange. They chose orange because it is the color hunters wear in the woods to protect themselves and others. Wear Orange began on June 2, 2015, Hadiya's 18th birthday, and is now observed nationally on the first Friday, Saturday, and Sunday of every June. This weekend is about solidarity, remembrance, and most importantly, action. Hadiya was killed more than a decade ago and 2,000 miles from my home in Oregon, but her story feels as close as ever. Our communities live with the perpetual sorrow of gun violence. Its shadow falls on our schools, our grocery stores, and our streets. Every single day, new families receive the devastating news that a loved one has been shot and killed. We can do more to prevent gun violence. We can ban assault weapons and bump stocks. We can ban the production of high capacity magazines. We can close background check loopholes. And we can stand up to those who profit by encouraging bloodshed. I am committed to taking on gun violence in our country. I am committed to protecting our communities. And I am committed to working for you. This weekend we wear orange to raise awareness of gun violence. But every day we act to try and end gun violence. Onward! Jeff | Express Donate: If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution instantly: | = | = | = | = ---|---|---|--- | | Paid for by Jeff Merkley for Oregon. | This email was sent by Jeff Merkley for Oregon PO Box 14172 Portland, OR 97293 United States Email is an important way for Jeff Merkley to keep supporters like you informed about critical issues and to build a winning grassroots campaign. _Democrat Eric Swalwell represents California's 14th Congressional District. _
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/64290973/409595946/-662322978?refcode=headerfooter-header&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS85MDcyNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiM2MxMTJmYi00ZjAyLWVlMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Q_up7RssacLwaSCRDNnsvFN3zwBDvCIegGmR3K34DaA=)
Team, I wanted to reach out to you directly because you deserve a pat on the back. Let's face it, a _**lot**_ happened during the month of May. From **Trump’s unruly CNN town hall, to DeSantis’ failure to launch and George Santos arrest,** MAGA Republicans continued to unleash mayhem last month. So, I thank you for powering us through **another month of MAGA mayhem.** In the midst of it all, people like you stepped up to support my fight of standing up against MAGA extremists in Washington. Look, I know how often my team floods your inbox, but I also know how **critical it is that we meet deadlines.** And during May, you did just that. _In fact, you helped us exceed many of my fundraising goals, friend._ The MAGA agenda may be unpredictable, but your support for me is not. I know how committed you are to fighting back against extremism. **And our next test is meeting the end-of-quarter fundraising goal at the end of this month.** Now let's get to work. Thank you once again, friend. — Eric Swalwell

[**June 3, 1781:** Jack Jouett, a lesser-known figure in American history who played a vital role during the Revolutionary War. Dubbed the "Paul Revere of the South," Jouett embarked on a daring midnight ride to warn Thomas Jefferson and the Virginia legislature of the approaching British troops. His heroic act helped save important leaders and ultimately influenced the outcome of the war.](https://click.historyoftheday.com/u/click?_t=930f96a60371422687dbefcfca24c32b&_m=52532182b6e5412cbe98e3e4fdda5f57&_e=xf7qelxD_hwdOiQcJ9BWDil9Br1Rm0v9hihISWSSoEI2-6p7IOsipGO0yYJ0mISdpQndLPkmqwSx4OWpANt9YXi--WDtfI0fIOMnqMLI72acaUP_qgXCdRpKty65l_HhAMm5EZOmFtvHSMuOy5_NnnvwukoLrePTsS53e5cfdmsSfH-KSajGeg4344RepJgf-4U599id7YO0sKKW7OU8QzIKaHI3N4bjK5qf_zomKMucT4Vh5jnCtJoOZ0lR_KmBjEuAzpI2CHpGE8Y9AAl4zHmGHhnL-62lypGnVSpuVXo%3D) **Source -[history.com](https://click.historyoftheday.com/u/click?_t=930f96a60371422687dbefcfca24c32b&_m=52532182b6e5412cbe98e3e4fdda5f57&_e=xf7qelxD_hwdOiQcJ9BWDil9Br1Rm0v9hihISWSSoEI2-6p7IOsipGO0yYJ0mISdpQndLPkmqwSx4OWpANt9YXi--WDtfI0fIOMnqMLI72acaUP_qgXCdRpKty65l_HhgBI83mkT4VJ1o5Vhn_wE_G0Co58nG79zpo-eoC8hW8s5OE-AVY80oBTHjYrUYUvx9mvyULyM2JpjVpxUFVyPjzWzQEVdmXsPLwcm3M0kh2hOrqRL6utSGFWpJXsRRVfY6qiBvzemJwbXUYwd81EWZ71MEJm1bUAfwvqPZuJsE8g%3D)**

[**June 3, 1864:** The second battle of Cold Harbor commenced on June 3, 1864, following the Union's hard-won victory at Spotsylvania Court House. Union General Ulysses S. Grant encountered entrenched Confederate troops led by General Robert E. Lee near Richmond. After nine days of intense fighting, the Union lost 7,000 soldiers while the Confederates suffered 1,500 casualties. Grant proceeded to besiege Petersburg, marking the final major siege of the war. Eventually, Confederate forces relinquished Petersburg and Richmond on April 2, 1865.](https://click.historyoftheday.com/u/click?_t=930f96a60371422687dbefcfca24c32b&_m=52532182b6e5412cbe98e3e4fdda5f57&_e=xf7qelxD_hwdOiQcJ9BWDil9Br1Rm0v9hihISWSSoELeInYhTWZsHXD1LujOIruf6ow9aOn6EQEP3TckNrPzNPrW3tezVBDiDz7CUrTkmSXcKPxBiAqhAzF8b7-8CErzcSsF9jUahJg8YPxLW3tXaW5N91WNy6cbhFpgB7ckcA0X8D_tlTAJEiFVBoFSzIoo-5juhAcUCcnM7QdABRnYjw5phN-NuOAiAUenQSo0mMRylsftlQbvNZAQIZ5kHKS2) **Source -[loc.gov](https://click.historyoftheday.com/u/click?_t=930f96a60371422687dbefcfca24c32b&_m=52532182b6e5412cbe98e3e4fdda5f57&_e=xf7qelxD_hwdOiQcJ9BWDil9Br1Rm0v9hihISWSSoELeInYhTWZsHXD1LujOIruf6ow9aOn6EQEP3TckNrPzNPrW3tezVBDiDz7CUrTkmSXYXXxYuAw0zk3Ex85af8ysSPNCcIyVsx_ct2UbAvtptmUd4feg2R0FnYNve7HuG1zX0YbpWqlkPG4ik5PYCXOOSCBt1mEPseThBcc3v24V5IZ9fP5v1wn7gIWknHwKrhhQ7PRQQFnffwzrFHAgXs9Y)**

[**June 3, 2016:** Muhammad Ali, was a renowned boxer who became an influential figure both in and out of the ring. Ali's career spanned three decades, during which he achieved numerous victories and became the heavyweight champion of the world three times. Ali was known for his outspokenness, activism, and refusal to serve in the Vietnam War. His contributions to the civil rights movement and his impact on popular culture solidified his status as one of the most iconic and inspiring athletes in history.](https://click.historyoftheday.com/u/click?_t=930f96a60371422687dbefcfca24c32b&_m=52532182b6e5412cbe98e3e4fdda5f57&_e=xf7qelxD_hwdOiQcJ9BWDil9Br1Rm0v9hihISWSSoEKz7uPbIYUcX4vB4ePvZci0obtQTH3p6CkM1_iksFEYs8ck_uY04JZSIbYefgMOusx2JUfeEicznL--FVAFJuJheJOTvNCs1GSqwJ3CaJTKYLThJSmBe6OECng7ye5EJzMSlhQ01WPBWVwLWax84xuMWkDtYJBmIgBktmdZ1vd0fIA0xwq48dtZAsGJY0GCVyD2LRBYH3P2Mr-DiaO9o7CO8Ps95PHcBa5vxv2ES3UkA1GDyzrCMvwH3oTYY-8toFk%3D) **Source -[britannica.com](https://click.historyoftheday.com/u/click?_t=930f96a60371422687dbefcfca24c32b&_m=52532182b6e5412cbe98e3e4fdda5f57&_e=xf7qelxD_hwdOiQcJ9BWDil9Br1Rm0v9hihISWSSoEKz7uPbIYUcX4vB4ePvZci0obtQTH3p6CkM1_iksFEYs8ck_uY04JZSIbYefgMOusx2JUfeEicznL--FVAFJuJheJOTvNCs1GSqwJ3CaJTKYIz4F3p3z5lQqB0Pm_MgZsOcDpqHKxlWaXUbXkMpTIKxzq3_6WEHZxeyyb8ZVXQxXL7uPDReWsCJNTNDXod5YjaCX5rsV4NkAdlR5GLQ38PrJCZStKQsZ7ftiAQFLhYEo6sZYa1IjbbpOXMNE3a3PFU%3D)**

name: Minnesota Update (via DLCC.org)
body:
Minnesota has accomplished so much with our party in charge. If you support abortion rights, voting rights, LGBTQ+ rights, gun safety legislation, and more, then we urgently need you to join our team. | | |
---
| | Building an America that works for you and your family starts in the states. That’s why the DLCC invests 100% of our donations into our work to take down extreme Republicans, build Democratic power, and protect our democracy. Last November, we won against the GOP’s smear campaigns to gain a trifecta in Minnesota – flipping the Senate while re-electing Democratic Gov. Tim Walz. **It was a huge undertaking that required support from our entire team.** [Now, we’re fighting to flip more legislatures – and protect those we already hold from a Republican power grab – but our work is severely underfunded. So it’s more critical than ever that you chip in $7 to help state Democrats win » ](https://an.dlcc.org/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953yoKG8CTelWWDHki88pvURt3B-Jxc4nNvF-EVj5ZQXoufKxztxI-WzAeWDq9LwE3PrM4K2Yc5HlYfE-i__XtukVqWNFszR0UE0RqhcLZsnZVKQWQTpjoWiT5AsZvt0B6HI_-Upjn_0fjX16cz1BC6ZK5e8RliNJ22N3Dpl31sTwNau1vMjk8uKNmb1sFeV3rObLJ7IXcK9ZcxTqHDALRmYNwboUoe3vinyRCw-4aQV8P2PKNSCFL-Id4gZjeAecek1BUy8karUDZry4BEgBk27eeTLN1apIT0YsLjCa6vGZxn5SNzsQ0gTcgD50cZmUIv_Kusoq-CUnlq8SMSwUsnH8JLAhmVhodC6Nh3TmKVEa6IGqiamtYRyAc1ybChtOXvesY_sLRTc2RG22V6NI213T/3wq/FSTggdg-QcGw4QEjnTmZJA/h0/_QBKhluqUXyucWDBftqaZw2OzURX3-uKU1cbEknfAPg) Here’s just some of what the Minnesota legislature has achieved with our party in charge, and what Republicans would try to halt if they snatched back control: ✅ Established **abortion access as a fundamental right**
✅ Strengthened **voting rights**
✅ Provided **universal school meals**
✅ Set new energy benchmarks**to fight climate change**
✅ Approved 12 weeks of **paid family and medical leave**
✅ Banned conversion therapy and **strengthened LGBTQ+ rights**
✅ Passed commonsense **gun safety legislation**
✅ Created **tuition-free public college**
✅ Expanded **workers’ rights** That’s an impressive list, and it shows what’s possible when we elect Democrats and halt the Republican Party’s extreme agenda. **So if you support abortion rights, voting rights, universal school meals, the fight against climate change, LGBTQ+ rights, gun safety legislation, and more,_then we urgently need you to join our team._** [Can you chip in $7 right now to protect and build on our progress? We’re building Democratic power in the states one donation at a time, and we need your help.](https://an.dlcc.org/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953yoKG8CTelWWDHki88pvURt3B-Jxc4nNvF-EVj5ZQXoufKxztxI-WzAeWDq9LwE3PrM4K2Yc5HlYfE-i__XtukVqWNFszR0UE0RqhcLZsnZVKQWQTpjoWiT5AsZvt0B6HI_-Upjn_0fjX16cz1BC6ZK5e8RliNJ22N3Dpl31sTwNQZW3opd32TZ3W6vgpTMpeuwV0h1PlF54gM1JYvQ0iunGsLOKmPexTGSRGp6RHn8vwlxwJOXUIWlgGDXwhBKORJRiuljpAIzmUGiZgbDCSs-5yVc2_bJn93L5opof814pAlUFLh-VksbNO16pKSp0pF6qPGgMZafPBEY2QDLsCvoB4WBwPob6D3n07BOZVHnxA6s3KDE4dBcqbUjiB25RQhWBZv4NY0SZmXP_1bk-bjL/3wq/FSTggdg-QcGw4QEjnTmZJA/h1/KvBo7aDST3NMKfXZySQ98W4p_xsqg6u2swdcCOmVwPo) | If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process immediately.

clean_body: Minnesota has accomplished so much with our party in charge. If you support abortion rights, voting rights, LGBTQ+ rights, gun safety legislation, and more, then we urgently need you to join our team. | | | | | Building an America that works for you and your family starts in the states. That’s why the DLCC invests 100% of our donations into our work to take down extreme Republicans, build Democratic power, and protect our democracy. Last November, we won against the GOP’s smear campaigns to gain a trifecta in Minnesota – flipping the Senate while re-electing Democratic Gov. Tim Walz. It was a huge undertaking that required support from our entire team. Here’s just some of what the Minnesota legislature has achieved with our party in charge, and what Republicans would try to halt if they snatched back control: ✅ Established abortion access as a fundamental right ✅ Strengthened voting rights ✅ Provided universal school meals ✅ Set new energy benchmarksto fight climate change ✅ Approved 12 weeks of paid family and medical leave ✅ Banned conversion therapy and strengthened LGBTQ+ rights ✅ Passed commonsense gun safety legislation ✅ Created tuition-free public college ✅ Expanded workers’ rights That’s an impressive list, and it shows what’s possible when we elect Democrats and halt the Republican Party’s extreme agenda. subject: Mike Pence is jumping into the 2024 fray
disclaimer_text: Paid for by VPP
body:
URGENT: We need to know what you think ASAP!
| 🚨** _RED ALERT:_ Mike Pence is jumping into the Presidential race!** [](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/pence-approval/?source=VPP_20230603_EMPET_E3_T0_AC&t=6&akid=8110%2E878179%2EjCJ5ew) We’re worried voters will see Mike Pence as a compromise – a less extreme Trump.

**_But we know the truth that he’s just as bad as his former boss!_**

[**That’s why before Mike Pence launches his campaign, we need to know: Do you APPROVE of Mike Pence?**](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/pence-approval/?source=VPP_20230603_EMPET_E3_T0_AC&t=7&akid=8110%2E878179%2EjCJ5ew) | [NO, OF COURSE NOT! »](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/pence-approval/?source=VPP_20230603_EMPET_E3_T0_AC&t=8&akid=8110%2E878179%2EjCJ5ew)

**** 1,000 RESPONSES NEEDED BEFORE PENCE’S ANNOUNCEMENT ****

Thank you for your urgent response,

Voter Protection Project

(www.protectvoting.org)
Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. 401 2nd Ave. S, Ste. 303, Seattle, WA 98104

The Voter Protection Project is on an important mission: To end partisan gerrymandering and voter suppression by electing candidates at the state and federal levels that are forceful advocates for voting rights. Our enfranchisement agenda includes working to ensure that every state has automatic voter registration and online voter registration, convenient early voting, and independent redistricting commissions to ensure politicians are not able to draw their own maps.

Please, consider making a [one-time contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/121?t=1&akid=8110%2E878179%2EjCJ5ew). **Or, to make the most powerful lasting impact and help us oust Republicans up and down the ballot who attack our right to vote, make a[monthly sustaining contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/122?t=2&akid=8110%2E878179%2EjCJ5ew).**

name: Sen. Brown
subject: I want to hear from you
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY FRIENDS OF SHERROD BROWN
body:
| | |
---
Friend, if there’s one thing you should know about me, it’s this:
I’ve dedicated my life to fighting for the Dignity of Work – the belief that hard work should pay off for everyone, no matter who you are or what kind of work you do. For me, this means going to bat for working families in Ohio and across the country. It means making sure _your_ __ voice is heard in the halls of Congress and on the Senate floor.
I’m proud of the progress we’ve made in the last couple of years, including passing a historic infrastructure bill and standing up for workers’ rights. **But we’ve still got a long way to go, so as we chart out our next steps, I’m asking folks like you to weigh in.**
I’ve always said the best ideas don’t come from Washington – they come from the people we serve. In an ideal world, I’d get to sit down and chat with each and every member of this grassroots team to find out which issues are keeping you up at night, what makes you hopeful about our future, and what you want to see Democrats accomplish next. But since I can only be in one place at a time, our 2023 Priorities Survey is the next best thing:
[**So please, friend, will you take just a moment now to share your thoughts with me in our new priorities survey? Your unique perspective is critical to helping us defend this seat, keep the Senate blue, and continue making progress for working families.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/64294333/409597864/-176176352?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250&code=jtk241-su-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTMvMS84NzY0NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4ZWFhYTQ1YS01MjAyLWVlMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Vx6uUkBQi7CEiZCh5dG_TL3AmogfIxZXmbwDU9Gt2eQ=&emci=1186f43a-0000-ee11-907c-00224832eb73&emdi=8eaaa45a-5202-ee11-907c-00224832eb73&ceid=18332626)
| | [Take the Survey](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/64294340/409597865/-176176352?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250&code=jtk241-su-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTMvMS84NzY0NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4ZWFhYTQ1YS01MjAyLWVlMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Vx6uUkBQi7CEiZCh5dG_TL3AmogfIxZXmbwDU9Gt2eQ=&emci=1186f43a-0000-ee11-907c-00224832eb73&emdi=8eaaa45a-5202-ee11-907c-00224832eb73&ceid=18332626)
---
Thank you for taking the time. I’m looking forward to reading what you have to say.

With gratitude,

Sherrod Brown
**Please read on for a personal message from Sen. Sherrod Brown:** Friend – thank you for being a part of our grassroots movement and for sharing our commitment to the Dignity of Work.

Fighting for workers means building an economy that prioritizes the needs of workers over tax breaks for billionaires and corporations. No matter who you are or what kind of work you do, I promise to fight for you. But I need your help: Experts say I'm one of the most vulnerable senators up for reelection and Senate control could come down to Ohio. The only way we can defend this critical seat is with grassroots contributions from supporters like you.

**[If you're with me in this fight, please chip in any amount today to win in Ohio and defend our Democratic Senate majority.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/64294341/409597866/-1256022338?code=jtk241-su-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTMvMS84NzY0NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4ZWFhYTQ1YS01MjAyLWVlMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Vx6uUkBQi7CEiZCh5dG_TL3AmogfIxZXmbwDU9Gt2eQ=)**

Thank you!
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/64294344/409597867/-1256022338?code=jtk241-su-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTMvMS84NzY0NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4ZWFhYTQ1YS01MjAyLWVlMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Vx6uUkBQi7CEiZCh5dG_TL3AmogfIxZXmbwDU9Gt2eQ=)
| This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com.
[To unsubscribe from this email list, please click here.](https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/tClQUZwGoEOS_rmuR1aPwA2?unsubscribedata=hbkkmplWzUeQQ0ENwRf6j0uva6MQ2pzJyu8zp9Uywj3HDiOZ04P0EXTfmAU1MhDIJ93o1N8Mpygmx5H5Cnpd%2Bum3Vzk%2FrpMQ5m53BE613QCkzrOKmmkj3Im2q7ePgZ3SK8hpVjD9uJAuLl9vEoj2ddU7EPYf6%2FCkGWuWaRVuRji3BtM6yXaM%2FBYiOBalnnnAvi%2FX2GbfRcDaN%2FKAhadh1SwoXVShADNVqwNT6RFs%2BUSez%2F5rJ6RvSaSNUcUz4604Ul8SHfEGENUXACJrD%2F7EEw3KkyUgN%2FyA2xyz1COKD5mZ01E0Suldfu11U7IuP78eDEojoUFe47iAN%2F%2BHNpG2PPWBKObcvAX448Hv4iLkALHaopM38SsRjZBa%2B9QI09UoWs9FjVV4R1mD5nLNiXq00p77YRVKqHEg7GmvbxGClerXAUiZDQXcPtB4pFl8tirA) Friends of Sherrod Brown

name: Cliff Schecter, Blue Amp Action
subject: A June 20th primary means ensuring abortion remains legal in Virginia next year.
body:
Protect abortion in Virginia
| | | | |
---
| | |
---
| | | _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T21412811-4237-4d98-8aa6-bf9d798b1223/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._
---
|
---
| |
---
| | | | | Peter, **It’s getting close. June 20th is our chance to ensure abortion remains legal in Virginia next year.**_ _ _We only have a slim one vote pro-choice majority in the State Senate because_ _ __one of 2 Democrats who make up our majority, Joe Morrissey, is a "pro-life" Democrat who'll sign any awful anti-choice Republican garbage._ [**_But we have the perfect solution!__ __If you join us, in less than 3 weeks we'll replace the anti-choicer with progressive champion & former State Delegate. Laschrecse Aird, who's challenging Morrissey in the June 20th primary._**](https://default.salsalabs.org/T0d71521b-212a-495f-9c6a-f92695429ad0/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) _Aird is exactly the kind of candidate we must get behind. She grew up impoverished to a teenage mother and father who worked multiple jobs to get by. This motivated Aird to run for the VA House of Delegates. _ _In 2021, she was named Legislator of the Year by the Virginia Education Association. Aird was also lauded by the Virginia Chamber of Commerce for job creation. Two very different groups--both seeing Aird's __accomplishment and integrity. Now that's someone who can bring people together._ _Not surprisingly, when comparing Aird to Morrissey, this was the result:_ [](https://default.salsalabs.org/T1c457f52-9c1e-4ef7-a75f-d111e688bc3d/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) _Aird has made abortion rights the centerpiece of her campaign, running for "Roe Not Joe." She'll be taking that fight all the way to June 20th, but early voting has begun, so we must **SUPPORT AIRD** **NOW** or risk an anti-choice majority! _ [**_Will you join Blue Amp Action's own "Roe Not Joe," fundraiser, and support Lashrecse Aird with $5, $10, $25 or whatever you can?_**](https://default.salsalabs.org/T925771bc-06ba-4d59-8a63-e22503eec216/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) Thank you! Cliff

| | | About BlueAmp Action: We cannot sugarcoat the current situation. Right-wing authoritarianism, defeated by a worldwide coalition during WWII, was only in hibernation. Over the past decade it has infected democratic societies--including ours. It is as simple as it is terrifying: What we do now will define the future of our country and world. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal pols and pundits. **And savvy grassroots activists like you know, just like we do, that we can’t stop watching the people we help elect:_ after the elections, we have to keep organizing support for the policies we like and pushing back against the ones we don’t._** **__When the public tunes out between elections and the only people our elected officials hear from are the paid lobbyists of wealthy corporations, our government stops being accountable to the needs of ordinary families.__** **Democracy never sleeps, and neither can we.** So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in democracy, not dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do! Please follow Cliff Schecter on Twitter @cliffschecter, or subscribe to The Takedown with Cliff Schecter on YouTube, to keep up to date with this battle for the heart and soul of our country. — _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T04de90a0-6f84-4e18-9a07-5e3fd27e73e8/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._

Cliff | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Cliff Schecter President, Blue Amp Action Amplify Good ---|--- | | | | | Follow Us | | ---|--- | Blue Amp Strategies 1776 Mentor Ave. #411 | Norwood, Ohio 45212 513.438.0171 | info@blueampaction.com | Having trouble viewing this email? | | | | | | About BlueAmp Action: We cannot sugarcoat the current situation. Right-wing authoritarianism, defeated by a worldwide coalition during WWII, was only in hibernation. Over the past decade it has infected democratic societies--including ours. It is as simple as it is terrifying: What we do now will define the future of our country and world. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal pols and pundits. And savvy grassroots activists like you know, just like we do, that we can’t stop watching the people we help elect: after the elections, we have to keep organizing support for the policies we like and pushing back against the ones we don’t. When the public tunes out between elections and the only people our elected officials hear from are the paid lobbyists of wealthy corporations, our government stops being accountable to the needs of ordinary families. Democracy never sleeps, and neither can we. So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in democracy, not dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do! Please follow Cliff Schecter on Twitter @cliffschecter, or subscribe to The Takedown with Cliff Schecter on YouTube, to keep up to date with this battle for the heart and soul of our country. — You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can._ | | or

**GET TO KNOW VIVEK:** Vivek is an American entrepreneur, political and cultural thought leader, and New York Times bestselling author of **_Woke, Inc.: Inside Corporate America's Social Justice Scam_** , along with his second book, **_Nation of Victims: Identity Politics, the Death of Merit, and the Path Back to Excellence_**.

He has been a prominent and outspoken critic against ESG, discriminatory practices under the guise of critical race theory, and of what he calls "government-tech censorship," where the government colludes with big tech to do through the backdoor what it couldn't do through the front door under the Constitution.

Vivek is a first-generation American. Growing up in the Cincinnati area, he often recounts the sage advice he received from his father that "If you're going to stand out, then you might as well be outstanding." This advice set the course for his life: an accomplished pianist, a nationally ranked tennis player, and the valedictorian of his Jesuit high school. He went on to graduate summa cum laude in Biology from Harvard in 2007 and received his J.D. degree from Yale Law School in 2013, worked at a hedge fund, then started a pharmaceutical company, Roivant Sciences, where his team developed five drugs that went on to become FDA-approved.

In 2022, he founded Strive Asset Management, an Ohio-based firm, that takes direct aim at asset managers like BlackRock, State Street, Vanguard and others for their fiduciary breach of duty to maximize the wealth of their clients for Wokeism. Strive's mission is to restore the voices of everyday citizens in the American economy by leading companies to focus on excellence over politics.
